description: Create a new team organization on Hub
steps: Fill in required fields (see parameters below)

org_name.description: Becomes the URL slug — e.g. hub.getsolo.tech/your-org. Letters, numbers, and hyphens only. No spaces. Permanent — cannot be transferred.

display_name.description: Human-readable name shown in the UI

homepage_url.description: Optional external link for your organization

org_type.description: Select the type that best describes your organization

tags.description: Helps others understand what your org focuses on

expected_outcome: New org appears in your org switcher; you are its first Admin

validation.rule: Org switcher in nav shows the new org name; you appear in Settings → Members as Admin

common_errors: Email not verified — verify email first before creating an org
Org name taken — add a suffix like -team or -ai; names are first-come, first-served
Spaces in org name — use hyphens instead

description: Invite a user to join your team organization
steps: Go to Settings → Members inside your org context
Assign a role: Admin, Publisher, or Viewer

roles.Admin: Full access — manage members, billing, models, and audit logs
roles.Publisher: Create and manage models. Cannot access billing or member settings.
roles.Viewer: Read-only access to the org's catalog

expected_outcome: Invitee receives an email with a link to accept

validation.rule: Invited user appears in Settings → Members with a 'Pending' status

common_errors: Invite not received — ask them to check spam; resend from Settings → Members
Invitee has no Hub account — they'll be prompted to create one first, then land on acceptance page automatically
Invite link expired — resend from Settings → Members

steps: If not logged in, sign in or create an account
The org appears in your switcher immediately
expected_outcome: New org visible in your nav switcher

validation.rule: Org name appears in org switcher dropdown

common_errors: Link expired — contact your Admin to resend from Settings → Members

description: View the full activity history of your organization
steps: Ensure you are scoped to the correct org via the org switcher
explanation: Audit logs are read-only and cannot be edited. Only Admins can see this page.
expected_outcome: Chronological log of all org activity

validation.rule: Audit Logs page shows event history table

common_errors: Page not accessible — you may be a Publisher or Viewer, not an Admin. Check with your org's Admin about your role.
